headlines: Russia in the European energy sector
Thirty-one people are injured and several homes are destroyed after a magnitude 5.1 earthquake hits Paktika Province, Afghanistan. It is an aftershock of the June 22 quake.

events.text: Russo-Ukrainian War
2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine
Battle of Donbas
Russian shelling kills six civilians in Toretsk, Donetsk Oblast, as Ukraine says that Russia appears to be preparing for a new ground offensive near Sloviansk.
Russian occupation of Zaporizhzhia Oblast

Russia announces , former Prime Minister of Vologda Oblast, as the new Prime Minister in Zaporizhzhia Military-Civilian Administration. His appointment probably took place some days earlier.<ref></ref>
Russia in the European energy sector
Russia's Gazprom says that it "cannot guarantee" gas supplies to Europe due to "extraordinary circumstances" as it declares force majeure on gas supplies dating from June 14.
2022 European heat waves
2022 United Kingdom heat wave

The United Kingdom issues a "red extreme heat warning" as temperatures are predicted to reach a record  in parts of England. Wales reports a record temperature of  near the town of Hawarden, Flintshire, and the island of Jersey reports a record temperature of .
The Royal Air Force suspends all flights to and from RAF Brize Norton in Oxfordshire after the runway suffers damage due to the extreme heat.
Dublin, Ireland, reports a record temperature of . Parts of France also report record temperatures.
Sadiqabad boat sinking
Twenty people are killed and 30 others are missing after a boat capsizes in Sadiqabad, Punjab, Pakistan.
Thirty-one people are injured and several homes are destroyed after a magnitude 5.1 earthquake hits Paktika Province, Afghanistan. It is an aftershock of the June 22 quake.
COVID-19 pandemic
COVID-19 pandemic in mainland China
COVID-19 pandemic in Shanghai
Residents in nine of Shanghai's districts will be required to undergo COVID-19 testing until July 21 as part of an effort to reduce the community spread of COVID-19.
2022 monkeypox outbreak
2022 monkeypox outbreak in the United States
The Rhode Island Department of Health announces that the state has administered monkeypox vaccines to people who have had close contact with monkeypox patients.

Wild European bison are reintroduced to the United Kingdom for the first time in over a thousand years after three are released into a pine forest in Kent, England. [https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-england-kent-62164223 (BBC News]
2022 Ecuador prison riots
Thirteen inmates are killed and two more injured during a prison riot in Santo Domingo, Ecuador.
Four people are injured during a mass shooting at a nightclub in Marbella, Andalusia, Spain. The perpetrator is stabbed and hospitalized.
Russo-Ukrainian War
2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine

Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy dismisses Ukraine's prosecutor general Iryna Venediktova and the top security official of the Security Service of Ukraine, Ivan Bakanov. More than 60 officials from the agencies are alleged to be working with Russia. Additionally, 651 cases of alleged treason and collaboration are initiated against prosecutorial and law enforcement officials.
2022 United Kingdom government crisis
2022 Conservative Party leadership election
Tom Tugendhat is eliminated from the leadership race in the third round of voting.
2022 vote of confidence in the Johnson ministry
A motion of confidence in the Johnson government is passed by 347 votes to 238.
2022 Indian presidential election
The Indian parliament votes to elect the new President of India.
